# Transcrição: 02 How to Make Wordpress Sites GDPR Compliant

- **Fonte:** 02 How to Make Wordpress Sites GDPR Compliant
- **Duração:** 02:55

## [00:00 - 02:55]

Okay guys, so the first thing that you're going to want to do is make sure that you strip your website from any pixels. So remove the Facebook pixel from your header. And then what we're going to want to do is install this plugin called Cookie Notice by DeFactory. So when you're in the back end of your website, just search for plugins. And we'll go Cookie Notice. And we're going to click on Install. And then we're going to click on Activate. Okay, once we've activated Cookie Notice, we can go under the Settings and edit the settings that are under there. And so this is the default, I guess, warning or notice that it provides. I'm just going to leave it at that. I'm also going to provide people with a Read More. And I'm going to pull up the Privacy Policy here. I'll give you guys a link for that as well. But make sure you edit it and get a lawyer, da, da, da. Okay, now this is going to actually be blank for you here. So what we're going to do here is we're going to go into our ad account and we're going to pull up our pixel. And essentially what we're telling this plugin is that once someone accepts the terms, then we will be able to pixel them or cookie them. Okay, and then we're going to make it once they scroll, they're enabled the cookie, and once they reload the page, and we're going to make this infinity. All right. So the other thing here is that you can actually have this at the top or the bottom. I would do this at the top so it's a bit more prominent. And then we're going to click on Save Changes. And what we'll notice now if I refresh this WordPress site is there we go. Now we have the cookie notice and you'll see the pixel, there's still no pixel. Once I click OK or I start scrolling or I refresh the page, I am now pixeled. There you go. And this is basically how to be compliant with the new GDPR regulations. Make sure you have a privacy policy on your website as well. According to GDPR regulations, you're not supposed to cookie someone without their explicit permission. So this is a plugin to do that. Now if you are not wanting this to obviously because some people might bounce out or you might not be able to put some people in a custom audience that are not in the EU and that doesn't apply to them, the other plugin that you can use here is called Category Country Aware for WordPress. It actually works with cookie notice hand in hand. Once you activate this, you can basically click a button that says only activate cookie notice for European Union countries. You can even specify the specific countries that you want. So there you have it. This is how to basically set up a WordPress site to be GDPR compliant.
